Hardware Architecture¶

This section describes the physical hardware involved in the LED Stripe Controller system.

Architecture Element: Raspberry Pi Processing Unit ARCH_HW_RPI
status: open
tags: architecture, hardware
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_HW
links incoming: ARCH_HW_SPI

The Raspberry Pi (generation 3 or newer) provides:

  • A quad-core ARM Cortex-A53/A72 processor running Linux.

  • A hardware SPI controller accessible via /dev/spidev0.0 (and /dev/spidev0.1 for a second chip-select).

  • 3.3 V logic levels on all SPI pins.

The SPI peripheral supports full-duplex operation; for LED stripe use only MOSI (GPIO 10) and SCLK (GPIO 11) are required. CE0 (GPIO 8) is used as chip-select.
Architecture Element: SPI Bus ARCH_HW_SPI
status: open
tags: architecture, hardware, spi
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_SPI, ARCH_HW_RPI
links incoming: ARCH_HW_LED

The SPI bus connects the Raspberry Pi to the first LED in the stripe.

Signal

RPi Pin

LED Stripe

MOSI

GPIO 10

Data In (DI)

SCLK

GPIO 11

Clock In (CI)

GND

Pin 6 / 9

GND

A level shifter (3.3 V → 5 V) is recommended between the Raspberry Pi and the LED stripe to ensure reliable data reception.
Architecture Element: LED Stripe ARCH_HW_LED
status: open
tags: architecture, hardware, led
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_SPI, ARCH_HW_SPI
links incoming: ARCH_HW_POWER

The LED stripe consists of APA102 (or compatible) addressable RGB LEDs daisy-chained on a shared SPI bus. Each LED contains an integrated driver IC that latches its colour word on the rising edge of the clock.

Key electrical parameters:

  • Supply voltage: 5 V DC.

  • Current per LED (full white): ≈ 60 mA.

  • Maximum SPI clock: 20 MHz (datasheet); 8 MHz used for margin.
Architecture Element: Power Supply ARCH_HW_POWER
status: open
tags: architecture, hardware
links outgoing: ARCH_HW_LED

A dedicated 5 V power supply with sufficient current capacity shall power the LED stripe directly (not through the Raspberry Pi 5 V rail). The Raspberry Pi and LED stripe share a common GND reference.

ID

Title

Status

ARCH_HW_LED

open

ARCH_HW_POWER

ARCH_HW_SPI
